mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite unemployment spike, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with declining housing starts signals demand rotation away from capital investment toward consumption, reducing future productivity growth expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.5% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 2% month-over-month
failure_condition: Interaction term produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or the joint signal fails to outperform either component signal alone (Diebold-Mariano test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals supply-side weakness and rising unit costs, which compresses profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth combined with stable inflation signals a healthy labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth > 0.2% month-over-month AND CPIAUCSL growth < 0.3% month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in goldilocks periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1,200 thousand units annualized signal strong residential investment demand that supports construction employment and durable goods consumption, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances when HOUST > 1200, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation measured by CPIAUCSL combined with elevated federal funds rates signals monetary tightening that increases borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: Interaction term produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 0.3% month-over-month combined with rising payroll employment signals robust supply-side capacity expansion and productivity gains, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth > 0.3% month-over-month AND PAYEMS growth > 0.15% month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during productivity expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
